title: Transaction Manager Control
text: Transaction Manager Control TM-Controlled Logon If the logon path is controlled by a transaction manager, Uniface requests the following information for a new logon path: Transaction protocol used—Uniface calls the TM connector via the TM interface and requests the identification of the transaction protocol used by the TM connector. This allows support for various protocols, such as XA. Info (‘ I ’) information request—Uniface sends an ‘ I ’ request to the DBMS connector via the TM interface. The DBMS connector then indicates if it supports global transactions ( uinfo->TMCapable ). Global Transaction (‘ G ’) global transaction request—the DBMS connector is called with a new connector request. Via the TM interface, Uniface sends an indication of which protocol is being used, for example XA , and the DBMS connector returns information which the TM connector needs. TM logon—if the transaction manager has not already connected, Uniface calls the TM connector via the TM interface to connect to the DBMS. Logon (‘ L ’) logon request—via the TM interface, Uniface sends a logon request with uctrl->TMEnabled set to true which indicates that a logon connection already exists. The DBMS connector only needs to perform internal administration and DBMS configuration. The DBMS connector has to get the ‘logon handle’ for the DBMS connection. All further DBMS connector requests have uctrl->TMEnabled set, and no local transactions (ones which are not controlled by the transaction manager) are allowed. TM-Controlled Logoff The following steps occur during a logoff for TM-controlled paths: Logoff (‘A’) logoff request— uctrl->TMEnabled is set to true. The DBMS connector must perform any necessary internal administration, but it must not disconnect from the DBMS. The DBMS connector must stop using the DBMS logon handle it received during the logon request. For some DBMSs, this may mean releasing the handle. TM logoff—Uniface may call the TM connector via the TM interface to have the connector send a request to the transaction manager to disconnect from the DBMS. TM-Controlled Commit and Rollback When a path is TM-controlled, the commit and rollback requests are issued to the TM connector, which sends the request to the transaction manager. The commit and rollback requests are not sent to the DBMS connector. SQL Instructions If a TM-controlled DBMS connector is running, begin , commit and rollback instructions are managed by the transaction manager rather than the DBMS connector. When the ProcScript commands commit and rollback are issued, they are not passed to the DBMS connector but to the TM connector. If the path is TM-controlled, the begin , commit , and rollback instructions should not be used in the SQL workbench, nor in the sql ProcScript statement . Related Links uinfo Supplies Uniface with basic information about the DBMS and the connector. uctrl Exchanges general information between Uniface and the connector, and describes a table.
